title: GET /api/v2/search — account and hashtag search
description:
## What
Implement GET /api/v2/search returning account and hashtag results. Local persona search by username and display_name via LIKE query. Hashtag search from the post_tags table. No full-text post search in v1 — return empty statuses array. Support type param (accounts, hashtags, statuses), q param (query string), resolve param (fetch remote actor if @user@domain format), limit param.

## Why
Search is the primary discovery mechanism for clients. Phanpy and Elk both call /api/v2/search on their search UIs. Without it, users cannot find accounts or hashtags. Part of sub-epic 8-C.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social /api/v2/search?q=test&type=accounts and /api/v2/search?q=rust&type=hashtags to capture exact response shapes including the hashtag history array format; document all accepted query params and their defaults; check what resolve=true does for a @user@domain query.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add GET /api/v2/search handler; parse q, type, resolve, limit, offset, min_id, max_id params; for type=accounts or no type: query local accounts WHERE username ILIKE '%q%' OR display_name ILIKE '%q%', also check remote_accounts; if resolve=true and q matches @user@domain format, trigger actor fetch if not cached; for type=hashtags: query post_tags for distinct tags LIKE q, return with name/url/history (history=[]) array; for type=statuses: return {accounts:[], statuses:[], hashtags:[]}; serialize accounts as Account objects, hashtags as Tag objects.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests against in-memory SQLite with seeded accounts and post_tags rows; assert account search returns matching local account; assert hashtag search returns matching tags; assert statuses returns empty array; assert resolve=true for @remote@domain triggers fetch attempt (mock HTTP server); assert limit param respected; assert response shape matches mastodon.social fixture.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: curl mastodon.social /api/v2/search?q=gargron&type=accounts live fixture (not the code under test); mastodon.social /api/v2/search?q=rust&type=hashtags live fixture for Tag object shape.
- Test approach: integration test with in-memory SQLite; seed 3 local accounts with varying usernames; query for partial match; assert exact JSON shape of accounts[0] matches mastodon.social Account object structure; hashtag test: seed post_tags rows; search returns correct Tag array.

## Defensive programming checklist
- Sanitize q param: strip leading/trailing whitespace; reject empty q with 422
- LIKE query: escape % and _ in user input to prevent LIKE injection
- Limit param: clamp to [1, 40], default 20
- resolve=true: only resolve if q looks like @user@domain (regex check first), never resolve arbitrary URIs
- No .unwrap() in handler path
- Do not expose remote actor internal IDs in Account.id — use local snowflake ID
